readings.summary: Passover vigil: communal memory fuels courage and fidelity.

readings.citation:
Ps 33:1, 12, 18-19, 20-22. R. v.12b
Blessed the people the Lord has chosen to be his own.
readings.antiphon:
Ps 33:1, 12, 18-19, 20-22. R. v.12b
Blessed the people the Lord has chosen to be his own.

readings.summary:
Faith = trust‑in‑motion: Abraham obeys, sets out,
welcomes a promise beyond sight.

Stay awake and be ready!
For you do not know on what day your Lord will come.
The Master may arrive at an unexpected hour.

antiphons.text:
Look to your covenant, O Lord,
and forget not the life of your poor ones for ever.
Arise, O God, and defend your cause,
and forget not the cries of those who seek you.

O Jerusalem, glorify the Lord,
who gives you your fill of finest wheat.

antiphons.citation: Communion Antiphon • Option II |  Cf. Jn 6:51
antiphons.text:
The bread that I will give, says the Lord,
is my flesh for the life of the world.
